title: Cloud Monitoring: Qwik Start
aim: To monitor a Compute Engine VM instance using Cloud Monitoring — install agents, create uptime checks, alerting policies, and dashboards.

steps.title: Create a VM and Install Apache
steps.description: Create a lamp-1-vm with HTTP traffic allowed. SSH into it and install apache2 and php.

steps.title: Install the Ops Agent
steps.description: Run the Ops Agent installation script to start collecting metrics and logs from the VM.

steps.title: Create an Uptime Check
steps.description: Go to Monitoring > Uptime checks. Create a check for the VM on port 80 (HTTP).

steps.title: Create an Alerting Policy
steps.description: Create a policy based on CPU utilization. Set a threshold (e.g., > 0.5) and add an email notification channel.

steps.title: Create Dashboard and View Logs
steps.description: Create a custom dashboard with a line chart for CPU usage. Use Logs Explorer to view VM logs.

commands.purpose: Install a web server for monitoring
Install the Google Cloud Ops Agent

viva.question: What is Cloud Monitoring?
viva.answer: A service that provides dashboards, alerts, and uptime checks to monitor the performance and health of your cloud applications.

viva.question: What is the purpose of the Ops Agent?
viva.answer: It is installed on VM instances to collect system metrics (like CPU/Memory) and logs, and send them to Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging.

viva.question: What is an Uptime Check?
viva.answer: A test that runs periodically to see if a web server or service is responding correctly from different locations.

notes: The Ops Agent is the successor to the older Stackdriver agents.
